title: Attention Is All You Need
abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms.

title: GPT-4 Technical Report
abstract: We report the development of GPT-4, a large-scale, multimodal model which can accept image and text inputs and produce text outputs. GPT-4 exhibits human-level performance on various professional and academic benchmarks.

title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train on 1.4T tokens of publicly available data.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
abstract: We presents a general-purpose fine-tuning recipe for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). We combine pre-trained parametric and non-parametric memory for language generation.

title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
abstract: We explore how generating a chain of thought a series of intermediate reasoning steps significantly improves the ability of large language models to perform complex reasoning.

title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
abstract: We propose QLoRA, an efficient fine-tuning approach that reduces memory usage of LLM finetuning while maintaining full task performance.

title: Mistral 7B
abstract: We introduce Mistral 7B, a 7-billion-parameter language model trained on 8K context length that outperforms the best open 13B model (Llama 2 13B) across all evaluated benchmarks.

title: ColBERT: Efficient and Effective Passage Search via Contextualized Late Interaction over BERT
abstract: We propose ColBERT, a novel ranking model that employs a late interaction architecture to achieve the combination of efficiency and effectiveness in neural information retrieval.

title: FlashAttention-2: Faster Attention with Better Parallelism and Work Partitioning
abstract: FlashAttention-2 extends FlashAttention with even better parallelism and efficiency, achieving 2x speedup over the original implementation.

title: DeepSeek-V2: A Strong, Economical, and Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
abstract: DeepSeek-V2 adopts innovative architecture including Multi-head Latent Attention and DeepSeekMoE, achieving strong performance at a fraction of the training cost.

title: Mixtral of Experts
abstract: Mixtral 8x7B is a Sparse Mixture of Experts model that achieves comparable or superior performance to GPT-3.5 on most benchmarks while being fully open source.

title: RAGas: Evaluation Framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
abstract: RAGas provides an open-source framework for evaluating RAG pipelines using LLM-assisted metrics for faithfulness, answer relevance, and context precision.

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
abstract: AgentBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLMs as agents across diverse real-world software environments and tasks.

title: Tool Learning with Large Language Models
abstract: We present a comprehensive survey of tool learning with LLMs, covering the paradigm, evaluation, and applications of LLM-based tool learning systems.

title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
abstract: Self-consistency leverages the insight that a complex reasoning problem can be solved in multiple ways. We sample diverse reasoning paths and select the most consistent answer.
